Copywriting Blueprint – Session 13
Or
Copywriting Blueprint - Week 7 Session 1
The Tsunami-style 4 part close
A pitch is not a close, pitch moves into a close – the close is what happens after someone is
pitched. There isn’t a limitation on closes in a VSL or a webinar – You should have multiple
closes (18 ready to go). A copywriter cant know what hot button they hit with each close or the
amount of closes it takes to get someone to buy (in face to face sales the average closes needed is
4.5 to make a sale)
CopyPro has some / most of this process already built in
Most people only try to close once or twice in copy
“Let me show you something else” and close again
Close until there is nobody left to buy in a live situation
Most marketers only write one close inside of their webinar, VSL or sales letter… this is a
massive mistake (so true)
4 part close
Jon uses a 4 part close: This is a 4 part process with one simple transition phrase
This process leverages different aspects of the buyers psychological state since it isnt know what
action they will take at this point
A push pull process may be needed
There are 4 states targeted here (they range from subconscious to top of mind):
· 	Fear (Negative - Problems they have are brought up or FOMO)
· 	Future (Positive - They can see themselves using the product – future pace)
· 	Rights (This will be induced / leveraged )
· 	Smarts (This will be induced /leveraged)
5:00
Crossroads close
This close is one of the most famous when it comes to copy – its used a ton but it still has power
The set up is there are 2 roads to take and only one of the options is the smart one (smart road)
(this is a mix of 2 states fear and smarts – the reader feels smarter by answering yes and smarts
will used later)
The fear is choosing the wrong road. The key is to amplify the wrong road with the buyer. (In
CopyPro the wrong road is LEVERAGING ULTIMATE FEARS) – Paint a frightening picture
Jon’s method uses the Socratic method to illicit the greatest amount of agreement (get the YES)
– this is the duality mindset
7:00
Crossroads close copy
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 8 --

Right now you are standing at a crossroads. A decision is mandatory. And the decision you will
make you will live with for a lifetime.
To your left lies the road you have been on for far too long. Where do you think that road will
lead? Isn’t it wise to assume it can only lead to more of the same? Of course it is.
However, its even worse. If you keep going down the road you are on now, you may end up
__________(ultimate fear). It is safe to say you want more out of your life than that? Yes it is.
That’s why you need to turn and look to your right. There’s the other road… the road that leads
to __________ (primary goal)… to the life you and your family richly deserve to enjoy. To your
right is a road to hope and fulfillment. And I’m walking that road right beside you.
Will you join me on the road to __________ (primary goal)
(Call to action)
(A good resource for asking questions to get to Yes is the “Yes deck” from Igor Ledochoski –
there are multiple Yes questions to learn from this product – Million dollar Mike Morgan (M$M)
wrote the copy for this product originally https://hypnosistrainingacademy.com/the-yes-decks/ )
Jon breaks down the close
Right now you are standing at a crossroads. A decision is mandatory. And the decision you will
make you will live with for a lifetime (This has a tough stance for the buyer – long term
implications will be caused – the common person says you are at a crossroads and you have
2 decisions – this isnt visceral enough and it is weak copy).
To your left lies the road you have been on for far too long (the word “lies” isn’t a mistake –
the subconscious mind picks up on this). Where do you think that road will lead? Isn’t it wise
to assume it can only lead to more of the same? Of course it is (general leading of the idea for
an agreement from the listener / reader – more to the YES train).
However, its even worse. If you keep going down the road you are on now, you may end up
__________(ultimate fear) (Build up the fears right here for the buyer and paint a horrible
picture). It is safe to say you want more out of your life than that? Yes it is (to gain more
agreement) .
That’s why you need to turn and look to your right (visualization of the action as well as a
command – turn is a change of mind). There’s the other road… the road that leads to
__________ (primary goal)… (paint the positive picture) to the life you and your family richly
deserve to enjoy (this information is planted). To your right is a road to hope and fulfillment.
And I’m walking that road right beside you (this is as a guide so they know they are not alone
– be on the journey with them).
Will you join me on the road to __________ (primary goal) (this is an invitation to join me not
to make a purchase – its tough to tell someone no when they ask you to go on a journey
especially as a guide)
(Call to action)
Call to action notes:
· 	Keep it simple
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 8 --

· 	Its not a requests – tell the buyer what to do
· 	Click the button right now – the one that says yes I want to ___________ (Primary goal)
this is a micro commitment.
· 	The buttons on the page should say “order now” at least
Friction is the enemy in life, this is in marriages, relationships and even writing copy.
(look up the difference between reactance and resistance – Dr Erick Knowles – University of
Arkansas)
Not in the class
One of the best visualizations of this close in the movie the Matrix when Neo has to make a
decision to join Morpheus and the resistance. This is the part of the movie under the bridge at the
beginning where the internal bug is removed from Neos belly button.
The wording uses the left side as the negative frame. In movies the villains always enter from the
left. The wording for the correct option is labeled as right not only directional but also congruent
with making the right decision.
Back to the class
14:00
Future close – NLP based – Future paced
Transition right out of the fear close into the future close with this sentence: “ Now, lets look at
your future now that you have chosen the right road…
Now – is an NLP command just like “listen” “look” “stop” “start”
There are presuppositions in this copy
The entire close paints a picture of the prospects life in the future, but with all present tense
language (future pacing)
“Its going to be” is talking about a future event not like “today is awesome”
Walk them down the three results times (CopyPro)
· 	Short term
· 	Mid term
· 	Long term
Example
16:45
Now let's look at your future now that you’ve chosen the right road…
Can you believe it's only been 7 days? You woke up this morning feeling leaner, more energetic,
and that bounce as you got out of bed? You’re not the only one who notice it. That kind of
energy is contagious. (play on what others see when future pacing – this makes the visualization
more vivid and crates and audience)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 8 --

Now it's 3 weeks later… you caught a glance at yourself in a mirror and just for a moment you
swore it was someone else. “Damn, that’s a good looking… Oh, that’s me! “its ok to say it…
you look fantastic. You’re back in that pair of jeans you swore off a few months back, and you’re
Turning heads right and left.
Now its 60 days later, and can you believe your life? Your health and energy are at an all time
high. Your partner mentioned how you “look like you’re back in college”, and you think back to
yourself: “it all started when AI trusted my gut and said YES to Jon…”
Just like you’re trusting it now… and saying Yes to this future you!
(call to action)
This copy is conversational as if it is happening right now and then moved into the future short
term and then long term. Jon uses this conversation in his guarantee. We love to have people see
our progress and also complements.
20:00
The rights close
The transition right out of the future close into the rights close uses a frame reset (do something
strange to get the listener engaged):
“Let me ask you a question? How important is freedom to you? Can you put a price on it?”
“You” is used a bunch of times – Jon likes the 10% rule to use “you” in 10% of the copy
This section is all about the freedom to choose and how it is a fundamental human right (Your
right to choose)
This “rights conversation” touches on the deepest of core values, as well as a fear that most older
folks have (that their rights are being stripped away) having rights removed is something people
don’t want
All three closes are tied to one narrative
The copy:
Let me ask you a question:
How important is your freedom to you? Can you put a price on it?
If you value freedom the way I do, then let me share something personal with you.
I believe to my core is one of the greatest freedoms we both have.
…is the freedom to choose.
There are some who want to strip that freedom away from you. Many who want to force you
down the wrong road. You know this first hand because you have been on that road for some
time now…
So as you veer right today… down the right road… toward the future we both see for you…
remember this:
Today, you are exercising your divine right of choice. That’s something to celebrate and never
take for granted. Choose now and join me on that road:
(call to action)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 8 --

Breakdown
Let me ask you a question:
How important is your freedom to you? Can you put a price on it?
If you value freedom the way I do, then let me share something personal with you. (rapport)
I believe to my core is one of the greatest freedoms we both have.
…is the freedom to choose.
There are some who want to strip that freedom away from you. Many who want to force you
down the wrong road. You know this first hand because you have been on that road for some
time now… (its not your fault – without saying its not your fault)
So as you veer right today… down the right road… toward the future we both see for you…
remember this: (command)
Today, you are exercising your god / divine right of choice. (powerful) That’s something to
celebrate and never take for granted. Choose now and join me on that road:
(call to action)
If this is a live call talk about who has bought the product or who has left a testimonial for social
proof and momentum.
26:00
The smarts close
Transition right out of the choice close into the smarts close with the use of a tie down phrase:
“And speaking of smart choices”
· 	This section is all about the prospects intelligence and right to brag to his or her family
about making a wise choice
· 	It subtly frames any other choice as ignorance
· 	This taps into the logical side of the brain, but in an emotional way as we anchor the
loved ones and friends into the choice
Smart choices copy
… And speaking of smart choices:
Tonight is going to be a night to remember. When you sit down with your partner, your friend, or
maybe a coworker you look up to, you get to say something magical:
“Today I made a truly wise decision”.
Today I took a step towards the life and legacy I deserve”
“today I get a second chance”
Isnt that what we deserve? A second chance? I believe all honest hard working folk deserve a
second chance when it comes to _________ (goal) … don’t you?
Now, not everyone is smart enough to know when an opportunity has presented itself. Yet you
and I ARE… and we’ve seen what the next 60 days will look like for you.. and we’re smart
enough to take the right road this time.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

If this only gave you half of what I was talking about… wouldn’t it be worth what I am asking
you for?
Homework:
Finish the actions section of CopyPro
If there is a webinar finish that as well
Manually write out my own version of the 4 part close using the data inputted into Copypro. Try
to not copy Jon’s words in the copy. Look for ways to come up with my own. Use the transitions
or turns that Jon uses or use my own – post the work in Facebook group
Next week: The Samurai Q and A finale
34:00 – Questions and answers
Future pacing question – In the copy past tense verbs are used should they be future based verbs.
This depends upon the structure of the conversation and where the reader is at. To go backwards
needs past tense verbs into the future is going to use future verbs
Derek – Is this or that a good way to associate to a product or service.
· 	That wrong road vs this right road
· 	Those obstacles vs these obstacle
Does dissociating language have influence on the copy (see above example) – since the copy
goes by so fast there. They key is to be vivid for the reader so they feel like they are pulled into
the copy. The closer the reader can feel to the copy the better – this process can work well
38:00
LeAnn – there are always more then 2 choices in the crossroads close. This process is to give the
to opposite options.
1. 	Pick what you are doing now and live with the pain or problem
2. 	Choose a new and better path
Closing takes another state for sales to happen in copy – it’s a switch to be a closer
Scarcity

-- 6 of 8 --

Jon wrestles with scarcity
Copywriter blueprint closed down at 30 people – he stuck to his guns on this
What to do with an ebook - Ryan Diess has an answer to this for closing he makes an offer
Jon came up with 2 prices for ebooks. The click bank price and the Amazon price – This book
sells for 39 and on this page it is selling for 17, but this price will not last long.
Scarcity is hard for salespeople face to face – copywriters struggle with this too.
Work in the framework of ethical persuasion
44:15
Tinder closes – no answer here – Jon does know about the dating niche
46:45
Daniela – Wants to launch offer – wants Jon to go over the sales page with the Q and A – If
there is nobody else for the hotseat. The q and a is the perfect time to ask questions.
Tim – Destination and action page is the same thing. The webinar section is longer. This is a
good section to go through from all of the top webinar marketers.
Elaine – wants to do NLP training – Harland Kilstein has a course - Kenrick Cleveland teaches
NLP maxpersuasion.com (as a side note Igor Ledochowski has good training on NLP as well)
In copy you can hear the commands and questions when you know what to look for.
53:00
Big Jason Henderson uses the Creativity Switch from Kenrick (Kassina machine is needed).
Jason Used this product for one of his product launches for a big name company to crush it.
57:00
Judi - Doug Obrien is also a good person for slight of mouth – NLP language patterns
The crossroads close left side is the do nothing option for the buyer.
Get the person to 1 idea and 1 solution – 1 road to take is more difficult when there are multiple
(more than 2) roads to take.
Look through Sellerator to find more conversations about feelings of taking action. Getting
people to a decision takes core values, fears and the pre frontal cortex (logic close – smart for
making a decision)
Brenan – How long should each section of the 4 part close be used (the length of content) use the
length in the examples roughly and not much more.
Does the length vary on the offer – Jon varys the length of copy based upon the investment.
Jon used the best 4 closes he knows in the example on purpose. They didn’t take Jon long to
write since he knows the patterns. Once these patterns are used they are easy to build within the
framework.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 8 --

Sir Gary Halbert says to write out all ideas and then walk away from them for ideas – come back
later and assemble the content. Learn when to step away. If you are pushing too hard its time to
take a break
Jon had to write a pitch deck over the weekend (Oren Klaff is the master of pitch decks with the
STRONG method – intersectioncapitol.com or pitchanything.com)
1:05:00
